prompt: What time does breakfast in the canteen start?
options: Seven in the morning
Eight in the morning
Nine in the morning
explanation: The manager says breakfast in the canteen is from seven to nine in the morning, so it starts at seven.

prompt: Where is the dorm manager's office?
options: On the first floor, next to the laundry room
On the ground floor, room number one
In the canteen
answer: On the ground floor, room number one
explanation: The speaker says, "My office is on the ground floor, room number one."

options: On Monday
On Thursday
On Friday
explanation: The teacher says, "The homework is due on Thursday, not Friday," reminding students not to confuse the two days.

prompt: What is the test on Monday about?
options: Numbers and colors
Grammar rules
The textbook exercises
explanation: The teacher explains, "The test is about numbers and colors," and tells students to study the new words from that week.

prompt: Why will the library close later than before?
options: So students have more time to study before exams
Because the group study rooms are being repaired
Because the front desk staff requested it
So the library can save on electricity costs
answer: So students have more time to study before exams
explanation: The announcement states the library will close later "so that students have more time to study before exams."

prompt: What should a student do if they lose their student card?
options: Wait until the next semester to get a new one
Ask a classmate to lend them a card
Visit the front desk immediately for a temporary card
Study in the group study rooms instead
answer: Visit the front desk immediately for a temporary card
explanation: The speaker says, "If you lose your card, please visit the front desk immediately to get a temporary one."

prompt: According to the tour guide, what can students do at the sports center?
options: Register for their student visa
Play basketball or swim for a small fee
Eat meals from six in the morning
Attend physics and chemistry classes
answer: Play basketball or swim for a small fee
explanation: Tom mentions the sports center on the right, "where you can play basketball or swim for a small fee."

options: At the main gate
At the science building
Near the international students' office
At the canteen
explanation: Tom says the tour will "finish near the international students' office, where you can ask questions about registration or your student visa."

prompt: Why has the exam location been changed to the main lecture hall?
options: Because the usual classroom is being renovated
To accommodate both sections of the course at the same time
Because calculators are required for the exam
To give students access to their handwritten notes
answer: To accommodate both sections of the course at the same time
explanation: The lecturer explains the exam is moved because "we require additional space to accommodate both sections of this course simultaneously."

prompt: What should students do if they have a scheduling conflict with the exam?
options: Bring a calculator to office hours
Notify the department office by the end of the week
Skip the exam and take a makeup test automatically
Attend both office hours sessions instead
answer: Notify the department office by the end of the week
explanation: The lecturer says, "please notify the department office by the end of this week so that alternative arrangements can be made."

prompt: According to the lecturer, why is direction important when describing the train's motion?
options: Because distance cannot be measured without it
Because two trains covering the same distance in opposite directions behave differently
Because velocity is not related to direction at all
Because trains always travel in a straight line
answer: Because two trains covering the same distance in opposite directions behave differently
explanation: The lecturer notes that "two trains covering the same distance in opposite directions are clearly behaving quite differently," which is why direction matters.

prompt: What question does the lecturer ask students to think about before the next class?
options: How far does the train travel between two stations?
Can an object be accelerating even while its speed remains constant?
What is the difference between distance and velocity?
Why is motion difficult to observe in daily life?
answer: Can an object be accelerating even while its speed remains constant?
explanation: The lecturer ends by asking students to consider: "can an object be accelerating even while its speed remains perfectly constant?"
